summary: Dependency-confusion probe exfiltrates installer identity to oast.live on postinstall
details: Package version 999.9.2 under the generic `@open-banking` scope ships a `postinstall` hook that runs `node scripts/scream3gg.js` on every `npm install`. The script collects the installer's hostname, home directory path, username, and external IPv4 addresses, hex-encodes them, and embeds them as a subdomain label in an HTTP request to `<hex-data>.d8c6tjnqeoph2u2v4bi0npmwqk6eurn6b.oast.live` (an Interactsh out-of-band collaborator listener). The fetch causes DNS plus HTTP leakage of installer identity to an attacker-controlled OOB endpoint without user consent. The 999.9.2 version number and minimal package contents are consistent with a dependency-confusion attack designed to outrank an internal private package of the same name and trigger automatic installation inside target organizations.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
